blockdesc-gantryshaft-*: Powered track for Gantry Carriages. Build a straight line and drive the shaft to move linked contraptions.
blockdesc-contraptionsaw-*: Contraption tool head that cuts trees while the assembled contraption moves into them. It searches for the lowest connected log near the blade, cuts the tree once work completes, and deposits drops into storage blocks carried by the contraption.
blockdesc-contraptiondrill-*: Contraption tool head that mines stone, ore, soil, gravel, and sand while the assembled contraption moves into them. Active drilling adds extra SU load and deposits drops into storage blocks carried by the contraption.
vintagekinematics:block-handbooktext-vin:
The Contraption Drill is a moving tool head for gantry contraptions. Mount it on the working face of an assembled contraption and move the machine into stone, ore, soil, gravel, or sand. It mines while powered, adds extra SU load only while actively drilling, and sends drops into storage blocks carried by the contraption when possible.

Why Does This Exist?
At some point every engineer encounters a hill.

The hill has made a choice.

This machine helps it reconsider.
blockdesc-gantrycarriage: Carriage for assembling moving contraptions on a Gantry Shaft.
blockdesc-gantrycarriage-*: Carriage for assembling moving contraptions on a Gantry Shaft.
placefailure-requiregantryshaft: Gantry Carriages must be placed on a Gantry Shaft.
itemdesc-mechanicalbinder: Selection tool for binding blocks to a Gantry Carriage.
blockdesc-primitivesieve-*: Crank era sieve for whole gravel, sand, soil, and other pannable blocks. Input top/side; output bottom/opposite side.
vintagekinematics:kineticforgepress-lini: Tier 3 refractory lining installed. T3 heat ready.
No tier 3 lining. Sneak right click with {0} fired tier 3 refractory bricks to upgrade.
The Forge Press already has a tier 3 refractory lining.
vintagekinematics:bore-status-halted: Halted at depth {0}: bit cannot break the block below.
vintagekinematics:bore-halted: Halted: drill bit cannot break the block below.
vintagekinematics:bore-paused: Paused. Open the bore and click Start Drilling to resume.
vintagekinematics:geothermalbore-halted: Halted: pipe path is blocked by an unbreakable block.
vintagekinematics:geothermalsteamengine-: Missing adjacent tapped Geothermal Bore heat output.
placefailure-boreplaceonly: The bore must install this pipe.
heldhelp-wrench-kinetic-rotate: Rotate blocks or pick up VK machines
heldhelp-backpackflywheel-charge: Charge from powered shaft or place
vintagekinematics:heldhelp-wrench-kineti: Rotate blocks or pick up VK machines
vintagekinematics:heldhelp-backpackflywh: Charge from powered shaft or place
vintagekinematics:powereddrill-no-fuel: The drill needs coal, charcoal, or a charged flywheel backpack.
vintagekinematics:poweredsaw-no-fuel: The saw needs coal, charcoal, or a charged flywheel backpack.
vintagekinematics:blockhelp-treadwheel-r: Move to drive, sneak to exit
vintagekinematics:treadwheel-idle-info: Mount the wheel, then press movement to drive it. Sneak to exit.
vintagekinematics:flywheel-status:
Charge: {0:0}/{1:0}s ({2:0}%)
Mode: {3}
Bank: {4} flywheel(s)
Burst output: {5:0} SU
Estimated time: {6:0}s
blockdesc-shaft-*: Carries rotation between machines. Wrap in a frame to pass it through walls and floors.
blockdesc-handcrank-*: Right click to wind clockwise from the side it was placed on. Sneak right click winds counter-clockwise. Provides a short pulse of rotation to a small network.
blockdesc-cogwheel-*: Couples two parallel shafts and reverses their direction. Place on the side of a shaft.
blockdesc-largecogwheel-*: Meshes with a small cogwheel on a perpendicular axis to step speed up or down.
blockdesc-gearbox-x: Vertical gearbox. Redirects rotation between vertical and horizontal shafts.
blockdesc-gearbox-y: Redirects rotation between horizontal shafts that meet at the corner.
blockdesc-gearbox-z: Vertical gearbox. Redirects rotation between vertical and horizontal shafts.
blockdesc-gearbox-*: Redirects rotation between any two perpendicular shafts that meet at the corner.
blockdesc-kineticclutch-*: Toggleable shaft coupler. Right click to connect or split the shaft line.
blockdesc-kineticreverser-*: Toggleable inline reverser. Right click to switch between straight shaft and reversed output.
blockdesc-kineticactivator-*: Powered block activator. Takes rotation from the back and activates the block in front.
blockdesc-creativemotor-*: Spins forever with no input. Creative use only.
blockdesc-coalmotor-*: Burns fuel to spin a connected network. Right click any side to fuel it, or feed it with a funnel.
blockdesc-treadwheel-*: Player powered kinetic source. Stand inside and move to drive it.
blockdesc-flywheel-*: Directional kinetic buffer. Dark shaft is input; lighter shaft is output. Sneak right click to set burst output.
blockdesc-reinforcedflywheel-*: Three block wide heavy buffer. Wood shaft is input; metal shaft is output. Sneak right click to set burst output.
blockdesc-reinforcedchest-*: 32 slot reinforced storage. Compact, expensive, and friendly to belts, funnels, chutes, and hoppers.
blockdesc-doublereinforcedchest-*: 70 slot reinforced storage made from two reinforced chests.
blockdesc-bulkcrate-*: Improved crate which stores 64 stacks of one item type.
blockdesc-normal-generic-reinforcedchest: 32 slot reinforced storage. Compact, expensive, and friendly to belts, funnels, chutes, and hoppers.
blockdesc-normal-generic-doublereinforce: 70 slot reinforced storage made from two reinforced chests.
blockdesc-normal-generic-bulkcrate-*: Improved crate which stores 64 stacks of one item type.
blockdesc-counterweightdrive-*: Stores hand wound kinetic drive. Right click to wind; sneak-right click to release.
blockdesc-encasedshaft-*: A shaft sleeved in wood so rotation can run through solid blocks.
blockdesc-kineticquern: Powered quern. Input top/left; output bottom/right, relative to placement facing. Use funnels or belts moving away from output.
blockdesc-crusher-bronze-*: Bronze crusher head. Handles rock, ores through iron tier, and bauxite for refractory mix.
blockdesc-crusher-iron-*: Iron crusher head. Faster than bronze. Crushes ilmenite chunks to nuggets, then nuggets to powder.
blockdesc-crusher-*: The driven head of a crusher pair. Mount above a Crusher Basin and feed it with a shaft.
blockdesc-crusherbasin-*: Crusher work basin. Input top/left; output bottom/right, relative to placement facing.
blockdesc-belt-*: Powered conveyor between shaft endpoints. Right click with another belt to chain; empty hand right click to collect carried items.
blockdesc-funnel-copper-*: Slow filtered item mover. Pulls from the block above and pushes out the face it points at.
blockdesc-funnel-iron-*: Fast filtered item mover. Pulls from the block above and pushes out the face it points at.
blockdesc-trashcan: Filtered automation sink. Deletes matching items from belts and funnels only.
blockdesc-kineticsieve-*: Powered sieve for crushed rock, grit, dust, and pannable blocks. Input top/side; output bottom/opposite side.
blockdesc-kineticsawmill-*: Powered sawmill for logs. Input top/local west; output bottom/local east. Use non shaft faces for item IO.
blockdesc-kineticextractor-*: Powered organic extractor. Item input top/side; solid output bottom/opposite side; liquids drain into an open container below.
blockdesc-kineticforgepress-*: Fueled hot metal press. Metal and fuel input top/side; output bottom/opposite side. Bellows improve chamber heat.
blockdesc-kineticbellows-*: Powered bellows for forge presses and firepits. Point the nozzle at the target and supply rotation.
blockdesc-kineticmixer-*: Two block mixer. Drive the upper cog; item input side; output bottom/opposite side.
blockdesc-kineticbore-*: Heavy powered drill. Load drill rods in the GUI; drain mined output from the upper back center face.
blockdesc-geothermalpipe: Borehole pipe consumed by the Geothermal Bore as it installs a permanent heat tap.
blockdesc-copperpipe-*: Passive copper liquid pipe. Pumps move liquid through connected pipe runs in any direction; the pipes themselves do not store liquid.
blockdesc-copperpump-*: Powered directional liquid pump. The arrow points toward output; the opposite side is input.
blockdesc-geothermalbore-*: Powered bore. Load drill rods and geothermal pipe; it taps heat only after reaching bedrock.
blockdesc-geothermalsteamengine-*: Consumes water from adjacent liquid storage beside a tapped Geothermal Bore to produce about 65,536 SU at 16 RPM.
vintagekinematics:block-handbooktext-vin:
You have discovered that carrying water in buckets is annoying.

Pipes solve this by moving liquids from one place to another while you stand nearby looking very important.

How To Use
Place pipes between things that contain liquid and things that want liquid. Pipes are passive conduits: they do not store liquid or move it by themselves. A powered Copper Pump supplies the movement.

Place the straight pipe item and it reshapes automatically into straights, elbows, tees, crosses, and vertical runs based on connected pipes, pump ports, liquid storage, and world liquid endpoints. Wrenching or changing neighbors refreshes the connection mask.

Pipes do not create water.
Pipes do not destroy water.
Pipes merely point at a problem and say:

"Go that way."

Careful where you point them.

A badly planned pipe network is how engineers accidentally invent fountains indoors.

Only the straight pipe is crafted and dropped. Breaking any shaped pipe returns the same straight pipe item. A pipe network can run in all six directions, but a pump only searches a fixed range from its input and output sides, so long runs need another pump stage.

Water was sitting over there.

You decided it should be over here.

The pump exists to enforce that decision.

How To Use
Connect the pump to rotational power. Connect pipes to both sides. The arrow points toward output; the opposite pipe end is input.

The pump consumes 4 SU at 16 RPM, scales with RPM like other kinetic consumers, and moves about 0.1 L per second at 16 RPM.

On the input side it can draw from connected liquid storage or from world liquid blocks such as water. On the output side it only fills compatible liquid storage; pipes are not tanks and placed water blocks are not output targets. Existing storage contents are respected, so a barrel holding one liquid will only accept matching liquid. Multiple valid sources and sinks are tried round robin.

Right click the pump to open its liquid filter. The filter starts in blacklist mode, so an empty filter allows all liquids. Click the filter slot with a filled bucket or other held liquid container to use its contents as the filter liquid.

If nothing happens:
- check power
- check pipes
- check that there is actually liquid somewhere

The pump cannot move water that only exists in your imagination.

Use this to turn infinite world water into actual upkeep: pump from a source into a barrel or other liquid storage, then let machines such as the Geothermal Steam Engine consume from that storage.

The Primitive Sieve is a copper tier hand crank worker. Feed it gravel, sand, soil, or any vanilla pannable material and let the shaft do the panning for you.

IO
Input: top or side feed face. Output: bottom or the opposite side, so it sits cleanly on top of storage. If the sieve faces north/south, side input is east and side output is west. If it faces east/west, side input is south and side output is north.

Yield rolls match hand panning, but it processes whole blocks instead of hand panning 1/8 of a block at a time. The early reward is throughput, especially once geared up.

The Kinetic Quern grinds vanilla quern recipes and turns rock grit into finer dust that sieves at higher yield with no dead rolls.

Due to superior kinematic technology, forcing a cog into your old quern somehow unlocks much faster RPM. Jump on it to roleplay a cog, or hook it to VK rotation for much more predictable input and output.

IO
IO is relative to the direction you faced when placing it. Input is from the left side or top. Output is from the right side or bottom. Example: facing north while placing gives west input and east output.

Output faces push into adjacent inventories, including chests, vessels, funnels, vanilla chutes, and hoppers. A belt on the right side also works when it is moving away from the quern.

The Shaft carries rotation between machines. Put enough of them in a line and suddenly the problem that was over there is now spinning over here.

Shafts preserve RPM and direction along their axis. Use gearboxes to turn corners, cogs to couple neighboring parallel shafts, and encased shafts when you want rotation to pass through walls and floors without looking like your workshop is made entirely of exposed ankles.

Recipe annoying? Make a sawmill. It cuts logs into shafts so you can stop hand crafting the same wooden stick with ambitions.

Why use a gearbox?

Because sometimes the shaft wants to go north and your machine wants to go east.

The gearbox exists to mediate this disagreement.

Use it to redirect rotation between two perpendicular shafts that meet at the corner. It keeps the same RPM and stress math, but changes which axis the problem is allowed to spin on.

Put a gearbox in the crafting grid by itself to swap between the regular horizontal gearbox and the vertical gearbox.

This is a cog. Put it on the side of a shaft and it shares rotation with another parallel cog while reversing direction, because teeth are just wooden arguments about which way spinning should go.

Small cogs are the basic way to make neighboring parallel shafts talk to each other. They keep the same RPM and SU headroom, but flip rotation direction across the mesh.

This is a cog. But bigger.

If the corner teeth of a big cog and a little cog touch, crazy things happen. The small cog and large cog mesh diagonally on perpendicular axes, turning one shaft into another while changing the ratio: small into large gears down, large into small gears up.

Use this when you want more RPM from a slow source, or when you need to trade speed back down for more usable stress headroom. If the tooltip says overstressed after gearing up, the machine is not confused; you asked the little teeth to believe too hard.

Recipe annoying? Make a sawmill. It cuts cogwheel sections so future big cogs hurt your patience less.

The Crusher Basin holds the item being crushed by a crusher head above it. It crushes raw rock and stones into grit, and graded ore blocks from poor through bountiful into enriched ore nuggets. Bronze crusher heads work through iron tier materials and can make crushed bauxite for tier 1 and tier 2 refractory mix. Iron crusher heads handle every crusher tier. For ilmenite, crush ore chunks into nuggets, then crush nuggets into crushed ilmenite for tier 3 refractory mix.

IO
IO is relative to the direction you faced when placing it. Input is from the left side or top. Output is from the right side or bottom. Output faces push into adjacent inventories, including chests, vessels, funnels, vanilla chutes, and hoppers. A belt on the right side also works when it is moving away from the basin.

Belts move items while powered. Right click a shaft with another belt to chain parallel shaft endpoints. Right click an empty handed belt to scoop every carried item on the chain into your inventory.

For controlled belt input, use a funnel. Place storage above the funnel, face the funnel toward the belt or machine, and use filters when you need only certain items to move. Belts are transport, not storage buffers.

A belt automatically picks up from a machine output side it runs past, deposits into a machine input side, and feeds a chest, vessel, or other storage placed at its end. Machine output faces can also push into adjacent inventories, funnels, vanilla chutes, and hoppers. Belt output requires the belt to move away from the machine output face.

Items stop at a solid block in their path and wait until the exit clears or a side funnel pulls them off.

A funnel is the normal active item mover for VK automation. It pulls items from the block directly above it and ejects out the face it points at. To drain a chest or vessel, place the funnel below the storage and face it toward a belt, machine input, inventory, or another target. Open the funnel to set whitelist, blacklist, exact, or fuzzy filtering.

Copper funnels are early, slow, and have fewer filter slots. Iron funnels are faster and carry more filtering capacity. Iron funnels can also be switched from continuous pulling to activator pulse modes: one item, half the current source stack, or the full current source stack per Kinetic Activator use.

Use funnels when you want to feed belts, feed machine inputs, meter items, or filter lines. Vanilla chutes and hoppers are useful passive output targets, but funnels are the intended controlled input for VK belts and machines.

The filtered trashcan deletes items inserted by belts or funnels from any side. It is not a normal inventory, so machine outputs do not push directly into it. Open it to set up to six whitelist or blacklist filter slots, with exact or fuzzy matching.

The Kinetic Sieve processes crushed rock, grit, dust, and vanilla pannable materials to recover sand, ore nuggets, gems, and rock-specific drops.

IO
Input is from the top or the side feed face of any drum segment. Output is from the bottom or the opposite side. If the drum faces north/south, side input is east and side output is west. If it faces east/west, side input is south and side output is north.

The Kinetic Sawmill cuts logs into boards, shafts, sticks, cogwheel sections, firewood, axles, angled gears, or horizontal gearboxes while powered. Choose the output type from its recipe browser.

IO
Input is from the top or local west side. Output is from the bottom or local east side. Local west/east rotates with the sawmill model, so attach belts and funnels to non shaft faces.

The Kinetic Extractor squeezes seeds, fruit, honeycomb, and similar organic inputs into oils, juices, honey, and solid byproducts while powered.

IO
Item input is from the top or local west side. Solid output is from the bottom or local east side. Local west/east rotates with the extractor model.

Liquid output is buffered inside the extractor, then drains into an open liquid container directly below it. Put a barrel below the extractor for honey, juice, or oil automation. Honeycomb returns beeswax as the solid output.

The Kinetic Forge Press is a fueled hot metal press. It heats metal, consumes rotation, and stamps plates, bloom output, dies, brackets, casings, pistons, collars, flanges, and alloy stock. After your first vanilla bloomery batch, it can smelt 20 limonite, hematite, or magnetite nuggets into an iron bloom, then refine that bloom into an iron ingot. It can also smelt vanilla non-iron metal nuggets into ingots without a ceramic smelting pot, and melt quartz, olivine, and quartz crystals into glass.

IO
Metal and fuel input come from the top or local west side. Output exits from the bottom or local east side. Local west/east rotates with the press model.

Bellows slots
The press has two bellows assist positions on the lower local-left side beside the item input. Powered Kinetic Bellows in those positions raise the maximum chamber heat, heat the chamber faster, and make stacked inputs heat much less painfully. Bloom smelting, glass melting, and titanium work are tuned around a two bellows press.

Bellows assist is pulse based and does not benefit from running at 256 RPM. Gear bellows down when possible; the press wants heat, but your SU line does not need to burn itself impressing the air pump.

T3 lining
Sneak right click the press with 24 fired tier 3 refractory bricks to install the high heat lining. The lining plus powered bellows reaches titanium heat and marks the T3 entry point.

The Kinetic Bellows helps the Kinetic Forge Press, Kinetic Charcoal Retort, and vanilla firepits. Place it beside a supported machine with the nozzle aimed at the multiblock. On the press, two powered bellows beside the lower local-left input side raise heat, speed chamber heating, and help stacked items heat more evenly. On the retort, two powered bellows beside the input side bring it to maximum speed. Point the nozzle at a firepit to accelerate burn and heating work.

Bellows do not need extreme RPM. If a bellows is already active, gearing it down is usually better for SU efficiency than running it at 256 RPM.

The in world tooltip reports whether the bellows is active and what target it is affecting.

The Kinetic Charcoal Retort turns firewood into charcoal at a 1:1 ratio while powered. It is the clean fuel-line partner for the coal motor: saw logs into firewood, cook firewood into charcoal, buffer the output, then feed the motor.

IO
Firewood input comes from the top or local west side. Charcoal output exits from the bottom or local east side. Local west/east rotates with the retort model.

Bellows and RPM
The retort does not get faster from 256 RPM. Its work speed is capped to the 16 RPM rate, then improved by bellows assist. Put up to two powered Kinetic Bellows beside its input side to reach maximum retort speed.

If the retort is powered and still feels slow, add bellows before adding RPM. If the bellows are already active, gear the bellows down to save SU.

The Kinetic Mixer is a two block powered mixer. Drive the upper cog cell with kinetic rotation; the lower basin does the mixing work. Its GUI has a 3x3 input grid and a 3x3 output grid.

IO
Solid inputs enter from the lower local west side only. Output exits from the bottom or lower local east side. Local west/east rotates with the mixer model. Slaked lime and water can be poured in by bucket or supplied through VK pipes.

Recipes
Mortar: put 2 rock grit and 1 sawdust in the mixer, then supply 1 L slaked lime to make 4 mortar.
Richer mortar: put 2 rock dust and 1 sawdust in the mixer, then supply 1 L slaked lime to make 6 mortar.
Dough recipes accept flour and pumped water in bulk.
It also automates dry blends such as plaster, blasting powder, raw refractory brick mix, and high fertility soil.

The Kinetic Bore is a heavy industrial drill. Feed it drill rods and rotation; it descends one block per cycle and leaves a 3x3 shaft behind it. Drill rods are returned when the bore retracts.

IO
Load drill rods in the GUI. Mined output is exposed from the upper back center face, opposite the front/shaft side. Put a funnel, chest, chute, hopper, or belt moving away from the bore against that back output face to drain it.

The Geothermal Bore is a tap installer, not a miner. Feed it drill rods, geothermal pipe, and rotation. Each completed work cycle deploys one drill rod and one geothermal pipe, then installs one pipe section in the center column below the bore. It destroys replaceable or minable terrain in that pipe path without producing drops.

When the next layer is bedrock or world bottom, the bore marks itself tapped and stops. Only a tapped bore provides heat to a Geothermal Steam Engine. Retracting removes the installed pipe column and returns both drill rods and geothermal pipes.

Why Does This Exist?
Because looking for surface resources is admitting defeat.

If the planet is refusing to give you power, simply drill into it until it changes its mind.

The Geothermal Steam Engine is the power block for a completed geothermal tap. Place its heat intake side against any cell of a tapped Geothermal Bore, then provide water from adjacent liquid storage such as a barrel. Placed water blocks do not feed the engine directly; use a powered Copper Pump and copper pipes to move world water into storage first. While heat and water are available, it runs at 16 RPM with 4096 source stress for about 65,536 SU.

Water use is 0.05 L per second. If water runs out or the tapped bore is removed or retracted, the engine stops and resumes automatically when the requirement is restored.

The Treadwheel is the sustained player powered source of the crank era. Stand inside the wheel and press movement to run in place; hold backward to reverse direction. Use it when hand crank bursts are too short or when an early crusher or sieve needs steadier power.

The Flywheel stores rotational energy while a treadwheel, counterweight drive, coal motor, or other strong source is spinning its input side. The dark shaft is input; the lighter shaft is output. Right click any connected flywheel to toggle the whole bank between charge mode and release mode.

In charge mode only the input side connects and it is a 1024 SU consumer at 16 RPM. In release mode only the output side connects and it becomes a 1024 SU source at 16 RPM for up to 180 stored seconds. Stored charge holds while the flywheel is installed in a valid, non-overstressed kinetic network. Sneak right click to set bank burst output to 1x, 2x, 4x, or 8x. Burst output multiplies SU and drains stored time at the same multiplier, so it spends stored work faster without creating extra work. Gear high RPM charging before the input side; gear high RPM output after the output side.

For forge press storage with 16x burst output, see the Reinforced Flywheel.

The Reinforced Flywheel is a forge press era 1x3x3 kinetic buffer with metal rim mass and bearing parts. Place the bottom middle cell; the shaft line is one block above it. The wood shaft is input and the metal shaft is output.

In charge mode only the input side connects and it is a 10240 SU consumer at 16 RPM. In release mode only the output side connects and it becomes a 10240 SU source at 16 RPM for up to 900 stored seconds. Stored charge holds while the bank is installed in a valid, non overstressed kinetic network. Sneak right click to set bank burst output from 1x up to 16x. Burst output multiplies SU and drains stored time at the same multiplier. Connected same axis flywheels toggle and configure together as one bank.

For the smaller crank era version, see the Flywheel.

The Reinforced Chest is a 32 slot post forge storage block. It costs a normal chest plus pressed bands, a shaft collar, planks, and a kinetic alloy plate, so it is a compact workshop upgrade rather than a cheap early chest replacement.

Use it as a direct output buffer for machines, belts, funnels, vanilla chutes, and hoppers. For one item type in high volume, use a Reinforced Crate.

The Double Reinforced Chest is a 1x2 reinforced storage block with 70 slots. Craft it from two Reinforced Chests when a workshop buffer needs more mixed item space without becoming a bulk crate.

Either half opens the same inventory. Machine outputs, belts, funnels, vanilla chutes, and hoppers can use it like other VK storage.

The Reinforced Crate is a 64 stack industrial crate for one item type. The first item inserted locks the contents, and the crate rejects other item types until emptied. It upgrades a vanilla crate with pressed casings, pressed bands, and kinetic alloy plates.

Use it for high volume output like grit, dust, planks, ore, stone, or repeated machine products. Adjacent machine outputs, belts, funnels, vanilla chutes, and hoppers can feed it as long as they are sending the stored item type.

The Kinetic Clutch is an inline shaft switch. When engaged it behaves like a normal shaft. When open it splits the kinetic network at that block, stopping rotation from crossing the clutch.

Right click to toggle it. Use it to isolate machines, stop gantries, or prevent two sources from conflicting.

The Kinetic Reverser is an inline shaft that can flip rotation across itself. In straight mode it passes rotation like a normal shaft. In reverse mode the chosen output side is inverted, so rotation crossing the block reverses direction.

Right click to toggle between straight and reversed mode. Place it facing the side you want to behave as the reversed output.

The Kinetic Activator consumes rotation and activates the block in front of it. Right click the activator to cycle modes: repeat while rotating, once until stopped, once per rotation direction, or pulse burst. Sneak right click cycles the activation delay: instant, 500 ms, 1000 ms, 1500 ms, 2000 ms, or 5000 ms. Ctrl right click cycles burst pulses: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 8, or 16.

Repeat mode waits that delay between activations. The one shot modes wait that delay after rotation starts, then activate once until stopped or once for that rotation direction. Pulse burst mode fires the configured number of pulses each time rotation starts, or when rotation direction changes. Blocks can opt into precise activator behavior through the VK kinetic activator API. As a first supported target, it can wind a Hand Crank in the direction of the activator's rotation.

Server configs can blacklist unsafe activator targets by block code, block class, block entity class, or entityClass name.

The Gantry Shaft is a powered track for constrained moving contraptions. Build a straight line of gantry shafts on one axis, then place a Gantry Carriage onto the line. Powering the shaft moves any assembled carriage along the track until it reaches the end, then it stops and can reverse when shaft rotation reverses.

This is the first contraption prototype, so keep builds modest and test them before moving anything valuable.

The Gantry Carriage attaches to a Gantry Shaft and carries a bound block assembly along that straight track. Place it on top of horizontal gantry shafts, or on any horizontal side of a vertical gantry shaft. Use a Mechanical Binder to add one or more selection boxes, then sneak right click the carriage with the binder to assign the connected blocks.

Right click the carriage to assemble or restore the contraption. Sneak right click cycles its placement mode.
vintagekinematics:item-handbooktext-vint: The Mechanical Binder selects the blocks a Gantry Carriage will carry. Left click one corner, then left click the opposite corner to add a selection box. Shift left click corners to add a removal box that subtracts from the selection. Repeat as needed, or right click to clear. While held, the binder highlights the selected non-air blocks; when aimed at a carriage it previews only the blocks connected to the carriage or its mount area. Sneak right click a Gantry Carriage to assign the selection. Loose blocks are discarded.

Make this billet in the Kinetic Mixer from one iron ingot and two charcoal powder. Heat it in an upgraded Kinetic Forge Press and run the Blister Steel operation to make blister steel, then refine that into steel.
vintagekinematics:block-handbooktext-vin:
The Counterweight Drive stores hand work as a timed burst of kinetic power. Right click to wind the weight, then sneak-right click to release the stored drive. It is useful for batches where you want the machine to run without standing at it the whole time.

Why Does This Exist?
Because batteries are complicated.

Rocks are simple.

Lift rock.
Drop rock.
Power achieved.

The bronze crusher is the early driven head of a crusher pair. Mount it above a Crusher Basin and feed it with a shaft. It turns raw rock and stones into grit, crushes ore blocks through iron tier materials into enriched ore nuggets, and crushes bauxite into crushed bauxite for tier 1 and tier 2 refractory mix.

Steel tier and harder pulverizer materials require the iron crusher.

The iron crusher is the faster version of the crusher head. Mount it above a Crusher Basin and feed it with a shaft; it handles every crusher and vanilla pulverizer tier at 50% higher speed than bronze. It is the VK route for high tier ilmenite processing after the Forge Press part chain: ore chunks crush into nuggets, and nuggets crush into powder for tier 3 refractory mix.
itemdesc-wrench-kinetic: Rotates compatible blocks. Sneak-right click encased shafts or cogs to remove their casing, or empty VK machines to pick them up.
itemdesc-casing-*: A wooden kinetic sleeve. Right click a shaft or cog to encase it; sneak-right click an encased block with a plank to change its wood.
itemdesc-belt: A leather strap. Right click 2 parallel shafts to form a powered conveyor.
itemdesc-beltcompound: A packed leather, resin, fat and rope laminate. Heat and cure it in the Kinetic Forge Press to produce belts in bulk.
itemdesc-sawdust: Fine wood waste from the Kinetic Sawmill. Used as fiber in mortar and future composite recipes.
itemdesc-crusherframe: A subassembly used to build the crusher.
itemdesc-largecogwheelsection: A subassembly used to build the large cogwheel.
itemdesc-pressedband-*: A hot-stamped metal band used for frames, tanks, oven doors and machine shells.
itemdesc-machinebracket-*: A hot-stamped bracket used to anchor shafts, frames and moving machine parts.
itemdesc-pressedcasing-*: A heavy stamped casing used as the shell of powered machines.
itemdesc-pistonhead-*: A stamped piston head for motors, compactors and other reciprocating machines.
itemdesc-shaftcollar-*: A collar that locks a shaft into a casing or bearing mount.
itemdesc-drillrod: A shaft reinforced with forge-pressed bands for the Kinetic Bore. The bore deploys one rod per depth layer and returns it when retracted.
itemdesc-pipeflange-*: A flanged connector for pipes, tanks, boilers and distillation equipment.
itemdesc-dieblank-*: An iron tooling blank for Forge Press dies. Heat to at least 700 C before precision working.
itemdesc-roughbearingblank-*: A rough bearing blank. Later machines can true or finish it into precision motion hardware.
itemdesc-industrialplate-*: A heavy structural plate for industrial machine frames.
itemdesc-boilerplate-*: A thick curved steel plate intended for pressure vessels and steam equipment.
itemdesc-valvebody-*: A stamped brass valve body ready for finishing into pressure and fluid controls.
itemdesc-kineticalloycharge: A packed stack of iron, copper, carbon and resin ready to be consolidated in the Kinetic Forge Press.
itemdesc-kineticalloyblank: A press consolidated alloy blank for post forge tools, frames and machine parts.
itemdesc-kineticalloyplate: A thin kinetic alloy plate for reinforced packs, storage shells and powered tool housings.
itemdesc-kineticalloyrod: A drawn kinetic alloy rod for powered tool shafts, linkages and compact mechanisms.
itemdesc-carburizedironbillet: Iron packed with carbon for upgraded Forge Press steelmaking.
itemdesc-poweredtoolassembly: A compact brass and alloy drive core for portable post forge tools.
itemdesc-drillhead-*: A forge pressed mining bit for a powered drill.
itemdesc-powereddrill-*: Fuel-powered drill with no durability loss while fueled. Open it to load coal or charcoal.
itemdesc-poweredsaw-*: Fuel-powered saw for cutting wood, plants, and leaves with no durability loss while powered.
itemdesc-pogorod: Spring mobility tool. Right click while grounded to launch. Hold right click while landing to rebound; simply holding it still absorbs fall damage. Sneak while landing to suppress the rebound. Sprint while airborne with a charged Flywheel Backpack to spend stored power on air sustain.
itemdesc-kineticboots: Spring-assisted boots that increase sprint speed by 25%.
itemdesc-reinforcedbackpack: A sturdy backpack upgraded with kinetic alloy framing. Provides 12 general storage slots.
itemdesc-backpackflywheel: A reinforced backpack cut down to 6 storage slots to make room for a compact flywheel. Sneak right click a powered shaft to wind it, or sneak right click the top of a block to place it as a cog-side kinetic charger.
itemdesc-pressdie-band: A reusable iron die for stamping hot ingots into pressed bands.
itemdesc-pressdie-bracket: A reusable iron die for stamping hot ingots into machine brackets.
itemdesc-pressdie-casing: A reusable iron die for stamping hot ingots into machine casings.
itemdesc-pressdie-pistonhead: A reusable iron die for stamping hot ingots into piston heads.
itemdesc-pressdie-collar: A reusable iron die for stamping hot ingots into shaft collars.
itemdesc-pressdie-flange: A reusable iron die for stamping hot ingots into pipe flanges.
itemdesc-pressdie-bearingblank: A reusable iron die for stamping hot ingots into rough bearing blanks.
itemdesc-pressdie-industrialplate: A reusable iron die for stamping hot ingots into industrial plates.
itemdesc-pressdie-boilerplate: A reusable iron die for stamping hot steel into boiler plates.
itemdesc-pressdie-valvebody: A reusable iron die for stamping hot brass into valve bodies.
vintagekinematics:item-handbooktext-vint: Make this reusable iron die in the Kinetic Forge Press from a heated Iron Die Blank. Use it in the press to stamp hot iron into Pressed Bands.
Make this reusable iron die in the Kinetic Forge Press from a heated Iron Die Blank. Use it in the press to stamp hot iron into Machine Brackets.
Make this reusable iron die in the Kinetic Forge Press from a heated Iron Die Blank. Use it in the press to stamp hot iron into Pressed Casings.
Make this reusable iron die in the Kinetic Forge Press from a heated Iron Die Blank. Use it in the press to stamp hot iron into Piston Heads.
Make this reusable iron die in the Kinetic Forge Press from a heated Iron Die Blank. Use it in the press to stamp hot iron into Shaft Collars.
Make this reusable iron die in the Kinetic Forge Press from a heated Iron Die Blank. Use it in the press to stamp hot iron into Pipe Flanges.
Make this reusable iron die in the Kinetic Forge Press from a heated Iron Die Blank. Use it in the press to stamp hot steel into Rough Bearing Blanks.
Make this reusable iron die in the Kinetic Forge Press from a heated Iron Die Blank. Use it in the press to stamp hot steel into Industrial Plates.
Make this reusable iron die in the Kinetic Forge Press from a heated Iron Die Blank. Use it in the press to stamp hot steel into Boiler Plates.
Make this reusable iron die in the Kinetic Forge Press from a heated Iron Die Blank. Use it in the press to stamp hot brass into Valve Bodies.

The Kinetic Wrench rotates compatible VK blocks. Sneak-right click encased shafts or cogs to remove their casing, or an empty VK machine to pick it back up. On belts, sneak-right click only removes an inserted middle shaft.

The Powered Drill stores coal or charcoal in its fuel chamber. While powered, it mines stone, ore, soil, and sand 25% faster than the same-tier hand tool and does not lose durability. If you wear a charged Flywheel Backpack, the drill draws from that stored rotation before lighting a new piece of fuel.

The Powered Saw uses the same fuel chamber as the Powered Drill, but cuts wood, plants, and leaves. If you wear a charged Flywheel Backpack, the saw draws from that stored rotation before lighting a new piece of fuel.

The Flywheel Backpack is a reinforced backpack cut down to 6 storage slots to make room for a compact flywheel. Sneak right click a powered shaft to wind it, either while holding the pack or with an empty hand while wearing it. Sneak right click the top of a block to place it as a kinetic charger; its cog side is the direction you are facing and only that side connects to a kinetic network. Breaking or wrench picking it returns the same backpack stack, preserving charge and contents. While worn in a backpack slot, its stored seconds power VK handheld tools before those tools consume coal or charcoal.

Why Does This Exist?
You asked:
"What if I carried industrial infrastructure with me?"

Everyone else said no.

You ignored them.

The Kinetic Pogo Rod is a post forge mobility tool. Right click while grounded to launch upward. Keep it in your active hand while landing to absorb fall damage, and hold right click while landing to rebound. Sneak while landing suppresses the rebound. If you wear a charged Flywheel Backpack, holding sprint while moving in mid-air spends stored flywheel seconds to sustain stronger air momentum.
Kinetic Boots use compact spring linkage to return part of each stride. Wear them in the foot slot to gain 25% sprint speed.
vintagekinematics:item-handbooktext-game: Make mortar in the Kinetic Mixer. Put 2 rock grit and 1 sawdust in the mixer, then supply 1 L slaked lime to make 4 mortar. Use rock dust instead of grit for the richer recipe: 2 rock dust, 1 sawdust, and 1 L slaked lime make 6 mortar.

Sift basalt grit in a Kinetic Sieve to recover sand and iron, copper, and tin nuggets. Grind it in a Kinetic Quern for finer dust with no dead rolls.
Sift bauxite grit in a Kinetic Sieve to recover sand, flint, copper nuggets, and blue clay. Grind it in a Kinetic Quern for richer dust.
Sift chalk grit in a Kinetic Sieve to recover sand and iron, copper, zinc, and lead nuggets. Grind it in a Kinetic Quern for finer dust with no dead rolls.

vintagekinematics:vkguide-progression-ov:
Vintage Kinematics Progression

Welcome to the land of insane engineers solving normal problems in absurd ways. Vintage Kinematics is its own kinetic power system: shafts, cogs, stress units, gearboxes, belts, machines, contraptions, and enough spinning parts to make a sensible person back away slowly. Vanilla windmills and water wheels can still feed VK through the bridge described in Kinetics for Dummies, but VK has its own progression path.

The short path
1. Build a hand crank, a shaft, and a primitive sieve. Congratulations: you have invented the spinning thing.
2. Put the sieve on storage and feed it gravel, sand, or soil. It processes whole blocks through vanilla panning drops, so a mountain of gravel can become a mountain of useful bits instead of a mountain of wrist pain.
3. For item automation, learn copper funnels early. Funnels are the intended active inputs for belts and machines: they pull from storage above them, push toward the side they face, and later provide filtering.
4. Decide whether you want to keep cranking, run in a treadwheel, or make your friends run in it. The counterweight drive is the next step: a rock on a rope that is somehow a serious power upgrade.
5. At bronze, build a bronze crusher over a crusher basin. Now stone is not waste; it is future metal dust with extra steps.
6. At iron, build the Kinetic Forge Press. This is where hot metal becomes plates, dies, brackets, casings, pistons, collars, flanges, and other parts that make the factory stop looking like wood tied to sticks.
7. Use Forge Press parts for the full kinetic sieve, iron crusher, sawmill, friction igniter, iron funnel, powered tools, and reinforced packs.
8. Stamp a pressed casing and piston head for the coal motor. This is the point where the machines keep working after you walk away, which is suspiciously close to civilization.
9. After steel and heavy bore parts, build the Geothermal Bore, Copper Pump, copper pipes, and Geothermal Steam Engine. Drill down, tap heat, pump water, and turn the planet into a power source.

Why the ladder matters
VK should make the vanilla climb more interesting, not delete it. Copper teaches motion and funnels. Bronze turns rock into a resource. Iron turns machinery into a part chain. Coal turns a workshop into a factory. Steel and geothermal power let you build projects that would be ridiculous by hand.

First target
If you are new, do not start by dreaming about geothermal steam. Start with a crank powered primitive sieve on top of a chest. When that works, gear it faster, add storage, then use copper funnels and belts to move items instead of hand feeding every machine. The central VK question is: how much machinery are you willing to build to avoid doing one chore manually?
vintagekinematics:vkguide-crank-era-text:
Welcome to Vintage Kinematics

The land where insane engineers solve simple problems in the most absurd ways possible.

Step 1: The Hand Crank
Congratulations. You can now spin a shaft.

It does absolutely nothing yet, but damn if it does not spin. Feeling ambitious? Throw some cogs into the line, change the ratio, and watch it go brrrrr. That is basically progress in this mod.

Step 2: The Primitive Sieve
Now give the spinning thing a job. Build a Primitive Sieve, put it on top of storage, and feed it gravel, sand, or soil. It uses vanilla panning drops, but it processes whole blocks instead of tiny hand-panned portions.

Take that giant pile of gravel and turn it into a slightly different giant pile of copper. You will need that copper to build more spinning things. This is peak early-game Vintage Story.

At this point you face the eternal question: do I really want to stand here manually cranking this thing like a medieval peasant?

Options include:
- A giant hamster wheel for personal use.
- A giant hamster wheel for your friends.
- Both.

The Treadwheel is sustained player power. Stand inside and move. Holding backward reverses direction. Putting a friend in it is not required, but it is hard to argue with results.

Step 3: Peak Early Game
Once you have enough copper and routing parts, you can gear your sieve faster. Push it hard enough and gravel will fear you. Storage, on the other hand, will become your mortal enemy.

Step 4: Advanced Technology
Eventually you unlock the Counterweight Drive, which is quite literally a rock on a rope. Wind it by hand, then sneak right click to release stored drive time.

Yes, a hanging rock produces more convenient batch power than a human in a wheel. No, we are not going to question the physics.

Welcome to engineering.

Bronze means crushing
Once you reach bronze, place a bronze crusher above a crusher basin. Rocks and stones become grit. Ore blocks become enriched ore pieces. That grit feeds the later full kinetic sieve, where ordinary stone starts turning into a real industrial metal line.

Other early workers
The Kinetic Quern handles quern work and can grind grit into richer dust later. The Kinetic Extractor handles organic pressing. Belts and copper funnels are where the build stops being one machine and starts becoming a workshop.
vintagekinematics:vkguide-forge-press-er:
Forge Press Era: Stamped Parts

The Kinetic Forge Press is where VK stops being wood, rope, and optimism. It burns fuel, heats metal, and uses rotation to stamp hot workpieces into useful parts. Plates, blooms, dies, brackets, casings, pistons, collars, flanges, bearings, drill heads, and alloy stock all start here.

What goes in
The press has slots for metal input, fuel, die, and output. Heat matters. Rotation matters. If either is missing, the press sits there judging your setup. Plates, bloom smelting, and iron bloom refining do not need a die. Most machine parts use reusable dies, because a proper factory should make the tool that makes the tool.

Use bellows for heat
A powered Kinetic Bellows beside the press makes the chamber heat faster and hotter. The press has two bellows assist positions on the lower local-left side beside the item input; fill both for high heat work and for heating larger stacks without waiting forever. Bellows do not benefit from 256 RPM, so gear them down once they are active to save SU. A bellows nozzle can also point at a vanilla firepit to speed burn and heating work. This is not just faster crafting; it is the start of heat engineering.

Post forge unlocks
Stamped parts turn iron into machinery instead of just better hand tools.
- Machine brackets unlock the full kinetic sieve, iron crusher, sawmill, and friction igniter.
- Pipe flanges upgrade item logistics through the iron funnel.
- Pressed casings and Piston heads are the coal motor bridge.
- Kinetic alloy plates and Kinetic alloy rods unlock rewards like the reinforced backpack, powered tools, and other questionable personal equipment.

Full rock processing
After the press, the full kinetic sieve becomes the industrial partner to the crusher and quern. Crusher grit gives rocktype keyed drops. Quern dust takes more work but gives richer siftable material. This is the point where mining stone on purpose starts making sense.

T3 lining
After the iron crusher unlocks ilmenite processing, make fired tier 3 refractory bricks and sneak right click 24 of them onto the press. The upgraded lining plus powered bellows reaches titanium heat. That opens titanium ingots and the Titanium Powered Drill: a very serious tool for people who have decided normal mining is over.
vintagekinematics:vkguide-coal-motor-era:
Coal Motor Bridge

The Coal Motor is the first sustained VK fuel source. It is intentionally post forge: build the Forge Press first, stamp a pressed casing and piston head, then assemble the motor. There is no all vanilla shortcut into fuel power. The factory must earn its engine.

Why it matters
Hand cranks, treadwheels, and counterweights spend player attention. The coal motor spends fuel. Once it runs, your crusher, sieve, quern, sawmill, bellows, extractor, belts, and funnels can work while you do something else, like build an even more unreasonable machine beside them.

Good first uses
- Keep a crusher, quern, and full sieve moving for rock to metal processing.
- Run bellows beside the Forge Press during long hot batches.
- Power belts and funnels so input and output buffers stop becoming chores.
- Use the sawmill to turn logs into boards, shafts, sticks, firewood, gearboxes, axles, angled gears, and cogwheel sections while the rest of the line runs.

Fuel is a project too
The coal motor makes fuel logistics matter. You can feed it coal, charcoal, and other valid fuels, but the strongly recommended partner is the Kinetic Charcoal Retort. It turns firewood into charcoal at a 1:1 ratio, which is a huge upgrade over charcoal pits when you want the motor to run without constantly clear cutting by hand.

The retort is capped to 16 RPM work speed. Do not gear it to 256 RPM expecting faster charcoal; use up to two powered bellows beside its input side to reach maximum speed, then gear those bellows down once they are active to save SU.

That makes the fuel line a real machine problem: sawmill for firewood, retort for charcoal, storage for buffers, belts and funnels to move it, then the coal motor to spend it. The goal is to keep the engine fed without becoming the engine yourself.

Player rewards after the press
The coal motor is not the only post forge goal. A reinforced backpack makes hauling less miserable, powered tools chew through work without normal durability wear, and the pogo rod exists for people who looked at walking and filed a complaint.

After coal power
The heavy path continues through the Kinetic Bore and then the geothermal chain. The bore is for people who want mining to be a machine, not a posture. The Geothermal Bore installs a permanent heat tap at bedrock. Pair it with a Geothermal Steam Engine and a pumped water loop for sustained high SU power.
vintagekinematics:vkguide-kineticsfordum:
Vintage Kinematics
If it spins, it works. If it does not spin, check your shafts.

VK is about turning annoying work into visible machinery. You do not get a magic button. You get shafts, cogs, belts, motors, saws, drills, pumps, and enough excuses to build something ridiculous.

Why use this instead of vanilla rotation?
Our sibling mechanics over in vanilla rotation do respectable, tame work. Mills grind. Wheels turn. The world remains mostly sane.

That is fine. We just are not that department.

Vintage Kinematics is for the engineer who wants belts carrying output, funnels sorting junk, machines chewing through rock, contraptions riding gantries, drills eating quarry faces, saws deleting forests, and power networks that loudly explain when your math has failed. Vanilla wind and water are still welcome guests through the bridge. VK is where they get handed a shaft, pointed at a factory, and told to keep up.

RPM and SU
Every kinetic network has two numbers. RPM is how fast it spins, including direction. SU is stress capacity versus stress demand. If demand is higher than capacity, the network overstresses and stops. If two sources fight over direction or speed, the network conflicts and stops. Machines are allowed to be dramatic, but the network is not allowed to lie.

Power sources
Early power is personal and slightly humiliating: the hand crank, the treadwheel, and the counterweight drive. Midgame power is fuel: the coal motor. Late power is industrial: the Geothermal Steam Engine fed by tapped heat and stored water. Vanilla windmills and water wheels can also feed VK if a vanilla mechanical-power block touches a VK shaft on the same axis.

Carrying power
A line of shafts carries rotation. A gearbox turns corners. A small cogwheel meshed with a large cogwheel changes speed and torque headroom. Gear up when you want speed. Gear down when you need capacity. If the tooltip says overstressed, the machine is not being stubborn; your math is losing.

Moving items
A belt moves items along a powered line. A funnel is the normal way to actively pull from storage, feed belts, feed machines, and add filters. It pulls from above and pushes toward the side it faces. Copper funnels are early, slow, and simple; iron funnels are faster and better filtered.

Machine outputs can push into adjacent inventories, funnels, chutes, hoppers, and belts moving away from the output face. Chutes and hoppers are useful passive output targets, but they are not the recommended belt feeder. For controlled belt input, use a funnel.

Your first useful build
Build a hand crank, shaft, and primitive sieve. Put the sieve on storage. Feed gravel. Crank. Then gear it faster and discover that output storage is part of the machine, not an afterthought.

Troubleshooting
Look at the tooltip first.
- Overstressed: add power, remove consumers, or gear down.
- Conflicted: two sources disagree. Disconnect one, clutch one, or reverse one.
- RPM zero: the shaft line is broken, the source is idle, or the machine is connected on the wrong face.

Build small, make it work, then make it excessive.
vintagekinematics:vkguide-contraptions-t:
Contraptions

Contraptions are for players who looked at a pickaxe, an axe, or a staircase and decided the correct answer was a moving machine. The current system is the Gantry Carriage running on Gantry Shafts. It moves in straight lines, which is already enough to build elevators, sliding doors, quarry faces, forest cutters, and deeply questionable platforms.

Track and power
Build a straight line of Gantry Shafts. The shaft line is the powered track, so drive it with normal VK shafts, gearboxes, cogs, motors, flywheels, or a vanilla bridge. Rotation direction decides travel direction. Hit the end, reverse the line, and the machine comes back like it meant to do that.

Placing the carriage
Place a Gantry Carriage directly on the gantry shaft. Horizontal tracks carry it above the shaft. Vertical tracks carry it on one horizontal side of the shaft. If placement fails, aim at the shaft face you want the carriage to grip.

Binding blocks
Use the Mechanical Binder to choose what the carriage carries. Left click one corner, then left click the opposite corner to add a selection box. Repeat for more boxes when the shape is not a clean cuboid, and use shift left click corners to subtract removal boxes from the selection. While held, the binder highlights the selected blocks; aim at the carriage to preview the connected set that will actually be captured. Selected boxes may include air, but the captured build must be connected by full block faces to the carriage or its mount area. Loose blocks are ignored. Gantry shafts stay in the world so your track does not leave with the train.

Running it
Right click the carriage to assemble or restore the contraption. While assembled, the selected blocks become one moving entity. Sneak right click the carriage to cycle placement behavior: always place when stopped, only place near the start, or only place when the anchor is destroyed.

Tools and storage
Contraption Drills mine terrain while moving. Contraption Saws cut logs and trees while moving. Their drops go into storage blocks carried by the contraption when possible. This is important: a quarry rig without crates is just an expensive way to throw rocks everywhere. Active tools add extra SU load only while working, so leave stress headroom on the gantry drive.
vintagekinematics:vkguide-machine-io-tex:
Machine IO

Machines are only impressive until the output sprays everywhere or the input starves. The usual VK rule is simple: input from the left side or top, output from the right side or bottom. Rotate the machine and those sides rotate with it.

How automatic input works
Inputs are strict. A machine will not magically pull from every nearby inventory. To feed an input automatically, use one of these paths:
- Belt moves items into the input face.
- Funnel points into the input face.
- Another machine output face pushes directly into that input face.

For active item movement, start with funnels. They pull from storage above them, push toward the side they face, feed belts, feed machine input faces, and provide filters. Belts then carry items along powered routes.

How automatic output works
Outputs are more forgiving. A machine output face can push into adjacent storage, funnels, vanilla chutes, hoppers, belts moving away from the output face, and compatible machine inputs. If a line is not moving, check whether the item is touching an input face or an output face first.

Standard framework IO
Most current powered machines follow the same model-local rule: top and local west are input; bottom and local east are output. Local west/east means the machine's modeled left and right sides after rotation, not fixed world west/east. A north-facing machine uses west input and east output. East-facing uses north input and south output. South-facing uses east input and west output. West-facing uses south input and north output.

Quern and Crusher Basin
These use placement relative left to right IO.

top input
v
left input -> [machine] -> right output
v
bottom output

If you faced north when placing the quern or basin, west is input and east is output. Top is also input. Bottom is also output.

Primitive Sieve and Kinetic Sieve
Input: top or side feed face. Output: bottom or opposite side. If the sieve faces north/south, side input is east and side output is west. If it faces east/west, side input is south and side output is north. A chest below a primitive sieve is the classic first setup.

Sawmill
Input: top or local west. Output: bottom or local east. Use the non shaft faces for item IO.

Extractor
Item input: top or local west. Solid output: bottom or local east. Liquid output stays in the internal liquid buffer for collection below.

Forge Press
Metal and fuel input come from top or local west. Output exits from bottom or local east.

Mixer
Solid inputs enter from the lower local west side. Output exits from bottom or the lower local east side. Liquids can be poured in by bucket or supplied by VK pipes.

Kinetic Bore
Load drill rods in the GUI. Mined output drains from the upper back center face, opposite the front/shaft side.

Liquid pipes and pumps
Copper pipes connect in all six directions and reshape to fit neighboring pipes, pump ports, liquid storage, and world liquid endpoints. Pipes do not store liquid. A powered Copper Pump pulls from the side opposite its arrow and pushes toward the arrow. Inputs may be liquid storage or world liquid blocks; outputs must be compatible liquid storage. For geothermal water upkeep, pump world water into a barrel or other storage, then place that storage adjacent to the Geothermal Steam Engine.

Chutes and hoppers
Vanilla chutes and hoppers work best as passive output targets when a VK machine or belt sends items into them. They do not actively pull from VK input slots by themselves, and they are not the intended way to meter items onto belts. Use funnels or belts for active feeding and filtering.
blockdesc-trebuchet-*: Burst powered player launcher. Set distance and angle, mount up, then launch from stored kinetic power.
vintagekinematics:block-handbooktext-vin:
"That is a really long walk."
- Someone who has clearly never built a trebuchet

You thought trebuchets were for flinging stones like some kind of medieval amateur. How quaint.

The Kinetic Trebuchet is a high tech, burst powered player launcher for serious engineers who get personally offended by walking across the valley.

How to Use
Sneak right click to open the launch settings. Choose your preferred distance and launch angle. We recommend probably fatal. Feed it enough stored kinetic power. Step into the basket and become the payload.

It demands a healthy pile of Stress Units (SU) at 16 RPM to operate, so pair it with flywheels or whatever other ridiculous power storage you have already over engineered.

Why Does This Exist?
Because walking is a failure of imagination.

Why spend five minutes strolling when you can spend three hours building a machine that yeets you across the map in 0.8 seconds while screaming?

Welcome to fast travel, Vintage Kinematics style.
vintagekinematics:trebuchet-tooltip: Launch target: {0:0} blocks at {1:0} degrees. Demand: {2:0} SU at 16 RPM.
vintagekinematics:trebuchet-tooltip-cont: Right click to mount. Sneak right click to set launch distance and angle.
vintagekinematics:copperpump-status-noso: No liquid source connected to the input side pipe run.
vintagekinematics:copperpump-status-filt: Connected liquid sources are blocked by the pump filter.
vintagekinematics:copperpump-status-nosi: No compatible liquid storage connected to the output side pipe run.
vintagekinematics:copperpump-status-bloc: Output storage is full or incompatible.
vintagekinematics:vkguide-logic-machines:
Logic Machines

Eventually a factory gets too large for the ancient engineering method of running over and right clicking things yourself. Logic machines are the parts that let rotation decide when a line connects, reverses, or pokes something in front of it. This is where the workshop starts developing opinions.

Kinetic Clutch
The Kinetic Clutch is a toggleable shaft coupler. In engaged mode it connects the shaft line. In disengaged mode it splits the line so power stops crossing through. Right click to toggle it by hand, or point a Kinetic Activator at it to toggle it with rotation. Use it for start and stop control, machine banks, safety shutoffs, and builds where one motor should not run everything all the time.

Kinetic Reverser
The Kinetic Reverser is an inline shaft that can flip rotation across its output side. In straight mode it behaves like a shaft. In reversed mode, rotation crossing the chosen side is inverted. Right click to toggle it, or let a Kinetic Activator toggle it. This is useful for gantries, belts, doors, and any machine where forward and backward are both part of the plan.

Kinetic Activator
The Kinetic Activator is the mechanical finger. Power enters from the back, and it activates the block in front. If a block has special VK activator support, that behavior is used. Otherwise it tries a normal block activation, unless the server config blacklists that target.

Right click the activator to cycle its mode. Sneak right click cycles the delay. Ctrl right click cycles burst pulse count.

Activator modes
- Repeat while rotating: activates every delay interval while powered. This is the simple poke forever mode.
- Once until stopped: activates once after rotation starts, then waits until the activator stops before it can fire again.
- Once per direction: activates once for clockwise rotation and once for counter-clockwise rotation. Useful when reversing a line should do something different.
- Pulse burst: fires a configured number of pulses when rotation starts or changes direction. Pulse count options are 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 8, and 16.

Delay settings
The delay can be instant, 500 ms, 1000 ms, 1500 ms, 2000 ms, or 5000 ms. Repeat mode waits this long between activations. One shot modes wait this long after rotation starts. Pulse burst waits this long between burst pulses.

Good targets
The activator can wind a Hand Crank in the direction of its own rotation, which means yes, you can build a machine that turns a crank for you. It can also toggle VK logic blocks such as clutches, reversers, and flywheels. That opens the door to mechanical loops, timed starts, direction-sensitive gantries, and other devices that look suspiciously like a factory thinking for itself.

Counterweight warning
No, the activator cannot operate a Counterweight Drive. The counterweight is intentionally a stored hand-work source: you wind the rock, then release the rock. If you want automatic sustained power, build a coal motor, use vanilla wind or water through the bridge, charge a flywheel, or go full geothermal. The rock on a rope is powerful, but it still demands a person with questionable priorities.
blockdesc-jsonmachinetemplate-*: Creative-only example of a fully JSON-defined kinetic processor. Feed it sticks while powered to produce hand cranks.
vintagekinematics:block-handbooktext-vin:
This creative only block is a working example of the generic JSON kinetic processor. It is a 1x2 multiblock machine using the standard VK modeling convention: shaft north south, west/top input, east/bottom output.

Its process recipe lives at vkrecipe/process/jsonmachinetemplate/stick-to-handcrank.json and converts one stick into one hand crank while the machine has rotation power.

Modders can copy the shipped template files under assets/vintagekinematics/templates/jsonprocessor/, rename the machine code, replace the shape, and add recipes under their own mod's vkrecipe/process/<machineCode>/ folder.
blockdesc-kineticsensor-*: Event based kinetic logic sensor. Takes rotation from the back and triggers the VK activatable block in front when its selected condition turns true.
vintagekinematics:kineticforgepress-stat: This metal and die do not match the recipe.
vintagekinematics:kineticextractor-io-to: Liquids drain into an open container below; solids output from bottom/opposite side.
vintagekinematics:block-handbooktext-vin:
The Kinetic Sensor turns machine state into a logic pulse

Give it rotation from the back. When its selected condition changes from false to true, it triggers the VK activatable block directly in front of it. Right click to toggle between overstressed network mode and storage full mode.

Modes
Overstressed network: triggers when the connected kinetic network becomes overstressed.
Storage full: watches adjacent inventories and triggers when every slot in any watched storage is occupied. Put the clutch, flywheel, or other activatable block in front of the sensor.

The sensor has no stress cost and does not pass rotation through. It only has one kinetic input side, so it should behave predictably even when logic lines get crowded.
vintagekinematics:bulkcrate-must-be-empt: Empty the Reinforced Crate before breaking it.
vintagekinematics:geothermalbore-must-be: Empty the Geothermal Bore before breaking it.
Retract the Geothermal Bore pipes before breaking it.
vintagekinematics:kineticbore-must-be-re: Retract the Kinetic Bore drill column before breaking it.
